name: Ted Heath
abstract: George Edward Heath (30 March 1902 – 18 November 1969) was a British musician and big band leader. Heath led what is widely considered Britain's greatest post-war big band, recording more than 100 albums, which sold over 20 million copies.

concerts.label: Dakota Staton, Singer, the Ahmad Jamal Trio, and the Ted Heath Orchestra
Midnight Performance: Dakota Staton, Singer, the Ahmad Jamal Trio, and the Ted Heath Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: Ted Heath even traveled with us... That was when the incident happened in Birmingham, Alabama... And then we had the group with Ted Heath and June Christie and Gary Morton and Nat.

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: was a Ted Heath record called “Live at the London Palladium” and the drummer was Ronnie Verrell. And to this day when I play that record I get excited.

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: we had our own wonderful band like Ted Heath for example, a wonderful big band, and Ted’s band kind of was a marvelous example of big band music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997
